question: Create an `Employee` class with `name` and `employeeID`. Then create a `Department` class that uses aggregation to manage a collection of `Employee` objects (e.g., using a `std::vector`). Implement methods in `Department` to add an employee and to list all employees within that department.

test_cases.expected:
--- Adding Employees to IT Department ---
Successfully added employee: Sarah Connor (ID: 301)
Successfully added employee: John Doe (ID: 302)
Successfully added employee: Jane Smith (ID: 303)
Attempting to add Sarah Connor again (ID 301)...
Error: Employee with ID 301 already exists in IT Department. Not added.
Successfully added employee: Mike Ross (ID: 304)

--- Listing Employees in IT Department ---
Employees in IT Department:
  ID: 301, Name: Sarah Connor
  ID: 302, Name: John Doe
  ID: 303, Name: Jane Smith
  ID: 304, Name: Mike Ross

--- Listing Employees in Empty Department ---
Employees in Empty Department:
  (No employees in this department.)
